aa: (noun) A form of lava flow associated with Hawaiian-type volcanoes, consisting of basaltic rock, usually dark-colored with a jagged and loose, clinkery surface. Compare pahoehoe.
aba: (noun) A coarse, often striped, felted fabric from the Middle East, woven from goat or camel hair.
abuzz: (adjective) Characterized by a high level of activity or gossip; in a buzz, buzzing.
ace: (noun) A single point or spot on a playing card or die.
ach: (noun) Any of several species of plants, such as smallage, wild celery, parsley.
add: (noun) An act or instance of adding.
adz: (noun) A cutting tool that has a curved blade set at a right angle to the handle and is used in shaping wood.
aft: (noun) The stern portion of a vessel.
ag: (noun) The art or science of cultivating the ground, including the harvesting of crops, and the rearing and management of livestock
aga: (noun) An honorific for high officials used in Turkey and certain Muslim countries.
age: (noun) The whole duration of a being, whether animal, plant, or other kind, being alive.
ago: (adjective) Gone; gone by; gone away; passed; passed away.
ah: (pronoun) The speaker or writer, referred to as the grammatical subject, of a sentence.
aha: (interjection) An exclamation of understanding, realization, invention, or recognition.
app: (noun) An application (program), especially a small one designed for a mobile device.
apt: (adjective) Suitable; appropriate; fit or fitted; suited.
ar: (noun) The name of the Latin-script letter R.
arb: (noun) The act of or potential for arbitrage.
arc: (noun) That part of a circle which a heavenly body appears to pass through as it moves above and below the horizon.
ard: (noun) A simple plough consisting of a spike dragged through the soil.
are: (noun) An accepted (but deprecated and rarely used) SI unit of area equal to 100 square metres, or a former unit of approximately the same extent. Symbol: a.
arf: (interjection) Representing the sound of a dog's bark.
ark: (noun) A large box with a flat lid.
arm: (noun) The portion of the upper human appendage, from the shoulder to the wrist and sometimes including the hand.
ars: (noun) The name of the Latin-script letter R.
art: (noun) The conscious production or arrangement of sounds, colours, forms, movements, or other elements in a manner that affects the senses and emotions, usually specifically the production of the beautiful in a graphic or plastic medium.
as: (adverb) To such an extent or degree; to the same extent or degree.
ash: (noun) The solid remains of a fire.
ask: (noun) An act or instance of asking.
ass: (noun) Any of several species of horse-like animals, especially Equus asinus, the domesticated of which are used as beasts of burden.
at: (verb) To reply to or talk to someone, either online or face-to-face. (from the practice of targeting a message or reply to someone online by writing @name)
auk: (noun) Any of several species of Arctic sea birds of the family Alcidae.
avo: (noun) A subdivision of currency, equal to one hundredth of a Macanese pataca.
bet: (noun) A wager, an agreement between two parties that a stake (usually money) will be paid by the loser to the winner (the winner being the one who correctly forecast the outcome of an event).
bey: (noun) A governor of a province or district in the Turkish dominions
bib: (noun) An item of clothing for people (especially babies) tied around their neck to protect their clothes from getting dirty when eating.
bid: (verb) To issue a command; to tell.
big: (noun) Someone or something that is large in stature
bin: (noun) A box, frame, crib, or enclosed place, used as a storage container.
bit: (noun) A piece of metal placed in a horse's mouth and connected to the reins to direct the animal.
bizzo: (noun) Business; a matter or matters of personal concern; a course of action.
buzzed: (verb) To make a low, continuous, humming or sibilant sound, like that made by bees with their wings.
buzzer: (noun) One who, or that which, buzzes; an insect that buzzes.
buzzes: (noun) A continuous, humming noise, as of bees; a confused murmur, as of general conversation in low tones.
by: (noun) A flying insect, of the clade Anthophila within the hymenopteran superfamily Apoidea, known for its organised societies (though only a minority have them), for collecting pollen and (in some species) producing wax and honey.
bye: (noun) The position of a person or team in a tournament or competition who draws no opponent in a particular round so advances to the next round unopposed, or is awarded points for a win in a league table; also the phantom opponent of such a person or team.
cad: (noun) A low-bred, presuming person; a mean, vulgar fellow.
cam: (noun) A turning or sliding piece which imparts motion to a rod, lever or block brought into sliding or rolling contact with it.
can: (verb) (auxiliary verb, defective) To know how to; to be able to.
cap: (noun) A close-fitting hat, either brimless or peaked.
car: (noun) A wheeled vehicle that moves independently, with at least three wheels, powered mechanically, steered by a driver and mostly for personal transportation.
cat: (noun) An animal of the family Felidae:
caw: (noun) The harsh cry of a crow.
cay: (noun) A small, low island largely made of sand or coral.
cel: (noun) A piece of celluloid on which has been drawn a frame of an animated film.
ch: (noun) A series of interconnected rings or links usually made of metal.
chi: (noun) The twenty-second letter of the Classical and Modern Greek alphabets.
cud: (noun) The portion of food which is brought back into the mouth by ruminating animals from their first stomach, to be chewed a second time.
cum: (preposition) Used in indicating a thing with two roles, functions, or natures, or a thing that has changed from one to another.
cup: (noun) A concave vessel for drinking from, usually made of opaque material (as opposed to a glass) and with a handle.
cwm: (noun) A valley head created through glacial erosion and with a shape similar to an amphitheatre.
czar: (noun) An appointed official tasked to regulate or oversee a specific area.
dab: (noun) A soft tap or blow; a blow or peck from a bird's beak; an aimed blow.
dag: (noun) A hanging end or shred, in particular a long pointed strip of cloth at the edge of a piece of clothing, or one of a row of decorative strips of cloth that may ornament a tent, booth or fairground.
dah: (noun) The spoken representation of a dash in radio and telegraph Morse code.
dak: (noun) A post system by means of transport relays of horses stationed at intervals along a route or network, carrying mail and passengers.
dal: (noun) Any of many dried husked pulses (legume), including peas, beans and lentils.
dam: (noun) A structure placed across a flowing body of water to stop the flow or part of the flow, generally for purposes such as retaining or diverting some of the water or retarding the release of accumulated water to avoid abrupt flooding.
dan: (noun) A title of honour similar to "master" or "father", used of historical and legendary figures of the past.
daw: (noun) A western jackdaw, Coloeus monedula, a passerine bird in the crow family (Corvidae), more commonly called jackdaw.
dee: (noun) The name of the Latin-script letter D.
def: (noun) Deficiency in amount or quality; a falling short; lack.
deg: (noun) A stage of proficiency or qualification in a course of study, now especially an award bestowed by a university or, in some countries, a college, as a certification of academic achievement. (In the United States, can include secondary schools.)
dor: (noun) A large European dung beetle, Geotrupes stercorarius, that makes a droning noise while flying
doxy: (noun) A sweetheart; a prostitute or a mistress.
dry: (noun) The process by which something is dried.
dso: (noun) A male hybrid of a yak and a domesticated cow.
dub: (verb) To confer knighthood; the conclusion of the ceremony was marked by a tap on the shoulder with a sword.
dud: (noun) A device or machine that is useless because it does not work properly or has failed to work, such as a bomb, or explosive projectile.
dug: (verb) To move hard-packed earth out of the way, especially downward to make a hole with a shovel. Or to drill, or the like, through rocks, roads, or the like. More generally, to make any similar hole by moving material out of the way.
duh: (interjection) Disdainful indication that something is obvious.
dui: (noun) Two people who work or collaborate together as partners; especially, those who perform music together.
duo: (noun) Two people who work or collaborate together as partners; especially, those who perform music together.
dux: (noun) The top (male or female) academic student in a school, or in a year of school; the top student in a specified academic discipline.
dye: (noun) A colourant, especially one that has an affinity to the substrate to which it is applied.
dzho: (noun) A male hybrid of a yak and a domesticated cow.
dzo: (noun) A male hybrid of a yak and a domesticated cow.
ean: (verb) To bring forth young; to yean.
ear: (noun) The organ of hearing, consisting of the pinna, auditory canal, eardrum, malleus, incus, stapes and cochlea.
eat: (noun) Something to be eaten; a meal; a food item.
ebb: (noun) The receding movement of the tide.
ecu: (noun) The European Currency Unit (symbol ₠), a currency used in the European Community before the euro.
era: (noun) A time period of indeterminate length, generally more than one year.
erg: (noun) The unit of work or energy, being the amount of work done by a force of one dyne applied through a distance of one centimeter. Equal to 10−7 joules.
erk: (noun) A member of the groundcrew in the RAF.
ern: (noun) A sea eagle (Haliaeetus), especially the white-tailed eagle (Haliaeetus albicilla)
ers: (verb) To utter the word "er" when hesitating in speech, found in the phrase um and er.
es: (noun) The name of the Latin-script letter S.
ess: (noun) The name of the Latin-script letter S.
eta: (noun) The seventh letter of the Modern Greek alphabet, the eighth in Old Greek.
eth: (noun) A letter (capital Ð, small ð) introduced into Old English to represent its dental fricative, then not distinguished from the letter thorn, no longer used in English but still in modern use in Icelandic, the IPA and other phonetic alphabets to represent the voiced dental fricative "th" sound as in the English word then. The letter is also used in Faroese, but is generally silent in that language.
eve: (noun) The day or night before, usually used for holidays, such as Christmas Eve.
ewe: (noun) A female sheep, as opposed to a ram.
ex: (noun) The name of the Latin-script letter X.
fil: (noun) A Nordic dairy product, similar to yogurt, but using different bacteria which give a different taste and texture.
fin: (noun) One of the appendages of a fish, used to propel itself and to manoeuvre/maneuver.
fir: (noun) A conifer of the genus Abies.
fit: (noun) The degree to which something fits.
fizz: (noun) An emission of a rapid stream of bubbles.
fizzes: (noun) An emission of a rapid stream of bubbles.
fly: (noun) Any insect of the order Diptera; characterized by having two wings (except for some wingless species), also called true flies.
fob: (noun) A little pocket near the waistline of a pair of trousers or in a waistcoat or vest to hold a pocketwatch; a watch pocket.
fog: (noun) A thick cloud that forms near the ground; the obscurity of such a cloud.
gay: (noun) (chiefly in plural or attributive) A homosexual, especially a male homosexual; see also lesbian.
gee: (interjection) A general exclamation of surprise or frustration.
gel: (noun) A semi-solid to almost solid colloid of a solid and a liquid, such as jelly, cheese or opal.
gem: (noun) A precious stone, usually of substantial monetary value or prized for its beauty or shine.
geo: (noun) (Caithness) An inlet, gully or cleft in the face of a cliff.
ghi: (noun) A type of clarified butter used in South Asian cooking; usli ghee.
gib: (noun) A bolt or wedge made from wood or metal used for holding a machine part in place.
gid: (noun) A disease of sheep caused by tapeworm.
gif: (noun) A bitmap image format for pictures with support for animations and up to 256 distinct colors per frame, including a fully transparent color.
gig: (noun) A performing engagement by a musical group; or, generally, any job or role, especially for a musician or performer.
gin: (noun) A colourless non-aged alcoholic liquor made by distilling fermented grains such as barley, corn, oats or rye with juniper berries; the base for many cocktails.
gis: (noun) The physical and behavioural characteristics of a bird that enable it to be immediately recognised by an experienced birder as a certain type of bird, especially to family or genus level.
git: (noun) A silly, incompetent, stupid, annoying or childish person (usually a man).
gnu: (noun) A large antelope of the genus Connochaetes, native to Africa, having curved horns.
gob: (noun) A lump of soft or sticky material.
god: (noun) A being such as a monotheistic God: a single divine creator and ruler of the universe.
hat: (noun) A covering for the head, often in the approximate form of a cone or a cylinder closed at its top end, and sometimes having a brim and other decoration.
haw: (verb) To stop, in speaking, with a sound like haw; to speak with interruption and hesitation.
hay: (noun) Grass cut and dried for use as animal fodder.
haze: (noun) Very fine solid particles (smoke, dust) or liquid droplets (moisture) suspended in the air, slightly limiting visibility.
hazzan: (noun) A Jewish cantor in a synagogue.
he: (noun) The game of tag, or it, in which the player attempting to catch the others is called "he".
hem: (noun) An utterance or sound of the voice like "hem", often indicative of hesitation or doubt, sometimes used to call attention.
hen: (noun) A female chicken (Gallus gallus), particularly a sexually mature one kept for its eggs.
hes: (noun) The game of tag, or it, in which the player attempting to catch the others is called "he".
hew: (verb) To chop away at; to whittle down; to mow down.
hi: (noun) The word "hi" used as a greeting.
hic: (interjection) An approximation to the sound of a hiccup, used e.g. to indicate drunkenness.
hid: (verb) To put (something) in a place where it will be harder to discover or out of sight.
highjacking: (verb) To forcibly stop and seize control of some vehicle in order to rob it or to reach a destination (especially an airplane, truck or a boat).
him: (pronoun) Honorific alternative letter-case form of him, sometimes used when referring to God or another important figure who is understood from context.
hin: (noun) A former Hebrew liquid measure of volume (about 3.8 L).
hip: (noun) The outward-projecting parts of the pelvis and top of the femur and the overlying tissue.
his: (pronoun) Honorific alternative letter-case form of his, sometimes used when referring to God or another important figure who is understood from context.
hit: (noun) A blow; a punch; a striking against; the collision of one body against another; the stroke that touches anything.
hmm: (verb) To make a thoughtful humming noise.
ho: (noun) A stop; a halt; a moderation of pace.
icy: (adjective) Pertaining to, resembling, or abounding in ice; cold; frosty.
id: (noun) The unconscious impulsive component of the personality in the Freudian psychoanalytic model.
ide: (noun) A freshwater fish of the family Cyprinidae, found across northern Europe and Asia, especially Leuciscus idus.
ids: (noun) The unconscious impulsive component of the personality in the Freudian psychoanalytic model.
if: (noun) An uncertainty, possibility, condition, doubt etc.
iff: (conjunction) If and only if; used to show that the truth values of two statements are the same.
jam: (noun) A sweet mixture of fruit boiled with sugar and allowed to congeal. Often spread on bread or toast or used in jam tarts.
jamb: (noun) (interior decorating) Either of the vertical components that form the side of an opening in a wall, such as that of a door frame, window frame, or fireplace.
jap: (verb) To carry out a sneak attack upon (something or somebody).
jar: (noun) An earthenware container, either with two or no handles, for holding oil, water, wine, etc., or used for burial.
jaw: (noun) One of the bones, usually bearing teeth, which form the framework of the mouth.
jay: (noun) Any one of the numerous species of birds belonging to several genera within the family Corvidae, including Garrulus, Cyanocitta, Aphelocoma, Perisoreus, Cyanocorax, Gymnorhinus, Cyanolyca, Ptilostomus, and Calocitta, allied to the crows, but smaller, more graceful in form, often handsomely coloured, usually having a crest, and often noisy.
jazz: (noun) A musical art form rooted in West African cultural and musical expression and in the African American blues tradition, with diverse influences over time, commonly characterized by blue notes, syncopation, swing, call and response, polyrhythms and improvisation.
jazzman: (noun) A male member of a jazz band.
jazzmen: (noun) A male member of a jazz band.
jeez: (interjection) An exclamation denoting surprise or frustration.
jerk: (noun) A sudden, often uncontrolled movement, especially of the body.
jet: (noun) A collimated stream, spurt or flow of liquid or gas from a pressurized container, an engine, etc.
jib: (noun) A triangular staysail set forward of the foremast. In a sloop (see image) the basic jib reaches back roughly to the level of the mast.
karzy: (noun) An outhouse or lavatory: a place used for urination and defecation.
kas: (noun) A spiritual part of the soul in Egyptian mythology, which survived after death.
kay: (interjection) Used to indicate acknowledgement or acceptance.
kea: (noun) Nestor notabilis, a parrot of New Zealand.
ked: (noun) Any of the family Hippoboscidae of obligate parasites, especially the sheep ked, Melophagus ovinus.
kef: (noun) A kind of cannabis smoked in Morocco and Algeria, for narcotic or intoxicating effect.
keg: (noun) A round, traditionally wooden container of lesser capacity than a barrel, often used to store beer.
ken: (verb) To give birth, conceive, beget, be born; to develop (as a fetus); to nourish, sustain (as life).
ket: (noun) A vector, in Hilbert space, especially as representing the state of a quantum mechanical system; the complex conjugate of a bra; a ket vector. Symbolised by |...〉.
key: (noun) An object designed to open and close a lock.
khazi: (noun) An outhouse or lavatory: a place used for urination and defecation.
ki: (noun) Ti, a plant native to the Pacific islands and China, Cordyline fruticosa.
kif: (noun) A kind of cannabis smoked in Morocco and Algeria, for narcotic or intoxicating effect.
kip: (noun) The untanned hide of a young or small beast, such as a calf, lamb, or young goat.
lee: (noun) A protected cove or harbor, out of the wind.
leg: (noun) A limb or appendage that an animal uses for support or locomotion.
lei: (noun) A garland of flowers in Hawaii.
lek: (noun) An aggregation of male animals for the purposes of courtship and display
les: (noun) A homosexual woman, one who is mostly or exclusively sexually or romantically attracted to other women.
let: (noun) The allowing of possession of a property etc. in exchange for rent.
leu: (noun) The unit of currency of Romania, equal to one hundred bani.
lev: (noun) The currency of Bulgaria, divided into 100 stotinki
li: (noun) The Chinese mile, a traditional unit of distance equal to 1500 chis or 150 zhangs, now standardized as a half-kilometer (500 meters).
lid: (noun) A thin skin membrane that covers and moves over an eye.
lie: (noun) The terrain and conditions surrounding the ball before it is struck.
lig: (verb) To lie; be in a prostrate or recumbent position.
lip: (noun) Either of the two fleshy protrusions around the opening of the mouth.
lo: (interjection) Look, see, behold (in an imperative sense).
lob: (noun) (ball sports) A pass or stroke which arches high into the air.
log: (noun) The trunk of a dead tree, cleared of branches.
loo: (verb) (used with at, upon or infinitive) To urge on with cries of loo or by other shouting or outcry.
med: (noun) (chiefly in the plural) Medications, especially prescribed psychoactive medications.
meg: (noun) Any unit having the SI prefix mega-.
met: (verb) To make contact (with) while in proximity.
mezuzah: (noun) A piece of parchment inscribed with specific Hebrew verses from the Torah (Deuteronomy 6:4–9 and 11:13–21) and attached in a case to the doorpost of a house, in accordance with the mitzvah (Biblical commandment as interpreted in Jewish law) to "write the words of God on the gates and doorposts of your house" (Deuteronomy 6:9).
mezze: (noun) Small portions of starters typical of Turkish and Greek cuisine (equivalent to Spanish tapas or Hawaiian pu pu) often served as a light meal with pita.
mho: (noun) A former unit of electric conductance, equivalent to and superseded by the siemens.
mi: (noun) A syllable used in sol-fa (solfège) to represent the third note of a major scale.
mil: (noun) An angular mil, a unit of angular measurement equal to 1⁄6400 of a complete circle. At 1000 metres one mil subtends about one metre (0.98 m). Also 1⁄6000 and 1⁄6300 are used in other countries.
mm: (interjection) An expression used to show contentment for something
mna: (noun) A monetary unit of ancient Greece and the Middle East, originally equivalent to the weight of a mina of silver.
mo: (adjective) Greater in amount, quantity, or number (of discrete objects, as opposed to more, which was applied to substances)
moa: (noun) Any of several species of large, extinct, flightless birds of the family Dinornithidae that were native to New Zealand; until its extinction, one species was the largest bird in the world.
mob: (noun) A large or disorderly group of people; especially one bent on riotous or destructive action.
mod: (noun) An unconventionally modern style of fashionable dress originating in England in the 1960s, characterized by ankle-length black trenchcoats and sunglasses.
moe: (noun) Strong interest in, and especially fetishistic attraction towards, fictional characters in anime, manga, video games, and/or similar media.
moi: (pronoun) (often used questioningly to express mock surprise) Me.
mol: (noun) In the International System of Units, the base unit of amount of substance; the amount of substance of a system which contains as many elementary entities (atoms, ions, molecules, etc.) as there are atoms in 0.012 kg of carbon-12. Symbol: mol. The number of atoms is known as Avogadro’s number.
mon: (noun) The former currency of Japan until 1870, before the yen.
ned: (noun) A person, usually a youth, of low social standing and education, a violent disposition and with a particular style of dress (typically sportswear or Burberry), speech and behaviour.
nee: (adjective) Used when giving the maiden name of a woman.
nef: (noun) An extravagant table ornament and container used in the Middle Ages and Renaissance, made in the shape of a ship.
neg: (noun) (pickup community) An expression or implication that one has a negative value judgement of someone in order to make them desire one's approval, especially when trying to pick up a date.
nek: (noun) A dip on a mountain ridge between two peaks.
nib: (noun) The tip of a pen or tool that touches the surface, transferring ink to paper.
nim: (noun) A game in which players take turns removing objects from heaps.
nip: (noun) A small quantity of something edible or a potable liquor.
niqab: (noun) A veil which covers the face, worn by some Muslim women as a part of sartorial hijab.
niqabs: (noun) A veil which covers the face, worn by some Muslim women as a part of sartorial hijab.
no: (noun) A negating expression; an answer that shows disagreement or disapproval
nod: (noun) An instance of inclining the head up and down, as to indicate agreement, or as a cursory greeting.
nog: (noun) A wooden block, the size of a brick, built into a wall, as a hold for the nails of woodwork.
noh: (noun) A form of classical Japanese music drama.
nom: (verb) To name someone as a candidate for a particular role or position, including that of an office.
non: (adverb) To no extent, in no way.
nor: (conjunction) And not (introducing a negative statement, without necessarily following one).
nos: (noun) A negating expression; an answer that shows disagreement or disapproval
oft: (adverb) (and in combination) often; frequently; not rarely
oh: (noun) An utterance of oh; a spoken expression of surprise, acknowledgement, etc.
ohm: (noun) In the International System of Units, the derived unit of electrical resistance; the electrical resistance of a device across which a potential difference of one volt causes a current of one ampere. Symbol: Ω
oho: (interjection) Expressing surprise or gloating realisation; aha.
ohs: (noun) An utterance of oh; a spoken expression of surprise, acknowledgement, etc.
oi: (noun) (sometimes capitalized) A working-class punk rock subgenre of the 1970s, sometimes associated with racism.
oik: (noun) A member of the lower classes.
oka: (noun) A former Turkish, Egyptian, Hungarian, and Romanian unit of weight, usually of a little more than a kilogram.
oke: (verb) To suffer pain; to be the source of, or be in, pain, especially continued dull pain; to be distressed.
old: (noun) (with the, invariable plural only) People who are old; old beings; the older generation, taken as a group.
ole: (interjection) An interjection used to stir up excitement.
olm: (noun) Proteus anguinus, a cave-dwelling neotenous salamander with external gills, found along the coast from northeastern Italy to Montenegro.
om: (noun) A sacred, mystical syllable used in prayer and meditation.
oms: (noun) A sacred, mystical syllable used in prayer and meditation.
oof: (noun) A sound made in pain, as when expelling air after being struck.
oom: (noun) An older man, especially an uncle. (Frequently as a respectful form of address.)
pad: (noun) A flattened mass of anything soft, to sit or lie on.
pah: (interjection) Used to express distaste, disgust or outrage.
pal: (noun) A friend, buddy, mate, cobber; someone to hang around with.
pan: (noun) A wide, flat receptacle used around the house, especially for cooking.
pap: (noun) Food in the form of a soft paste, often a porridge, especially as given to very young children.
par: (noun) A passage in text that is about a different subject from the preceding text, marked by commencing on a new line, the first line sometimes being indented.
pas: (noun) A pace; a step, as in a dance or in marching.
pat: (noun) The sound of a light slap or tap with a soft flat object, especially of a footstep
paw: (noun) The soft foot of a mammal or other animal, generally a quadruped, that has claws or nails; comparable to a human hand or foot.
pax: (noun) A painted, stamped or carved tablet with a representation of Christ or the Virgin Mary, which was kissed by the priest during the Mass ("kiss of peace") and then passed to other officiating clergy and the congregation to be kissed. See also osculatory.
pay: (noun) Money given in return for work; salary or wages.
pazazz: (noun) Flair, vitality, or zest; energy; vigor.
pe: (noun) The seventeenth letter of many Semitic alphabets/abjads (Phoenician, Aramaic, Hebrew פ, Syriac ܦ, and others; Arabic has the analog faa).
pea: (noun) A plant, Pisum sativum, member of the legume (Fabaceae) family.
pec: (noun) (usually in the plural) The pectoralis major muscle.
peg: (noun) A cylindrical wooden or metal object used to fasten or as a bearing between objects.
pen: (noun) An enclosure (enclosed area) used to contain domesticated animals, especially sheep or cattle.
pod: (noun) A seed case for legumes (e.g. peas, beans, peppers); a seedpod.
poi: (noun) The traditional staple food of Hawaii, made by baking and pounding the kalo (or taro) root, and reducing it to a thin paste, which is allowed to ferment.
pom: (noun) An Englishman, a Briton; a person of British descent.
pop: (noun) A loud, sharp sound as of a cork coming out of a bottle.
pot: (noun) A flat-bottomed vessel (usually metal) used for cooking food.
pow: (noun) A survey of people, usually statistically analyzed to gauge wider public opinion.
pox: (noun) A disease characterized by purulent skin eruptions that may leave pockmarks.
pro: (noun) An advantage of something, especially when contrasted with its disadvantages (cons).
psi: (noun) The twenty-third letter of Classical and Modern Greek and the twenty-fifth letter of Old and Ancient Greek.
pst: (interjection) Implies that the speaker is sending secret or whispered information to another person.
pub: (noun) A public house where beverages, primarily alcoholic, may be bought and consumed, also providing food and sometimes entertainment such as live music or television.
pug: (noun) A small dog of an ancient breed originating in China, having a snub nose, wrinkled face, squarish body, short smooth hair, and curled tail.
pul: (noun) A subdivision of currency, equal to one hundredth of an Afghan afghani.
pun: (noun) A joke or type of wordplay in which similar definitions or sounds of two words or phrases, or different definitions of the same word, are deliberately confused.
pup: (noun) A young dog, wolf, fox, seal, or shark, or the young of certain other animals.
pur: (noun) The vibrating sound made by a cat in its throat when contented.
pus: (noun) A whitish-yellow or yellow substance composed primarily of dead white blood cells and dead pyogenic bacteria; normally found in regions of bacterial infection.
put: (noun) A right to sell something at a predetermined price.
puy: (noun) Any of several cone-shaped hills in the Auvergne, France that are the remains of extinct volcanos
pya: (noun) A subdivision of currency, equal to one hundredth of a Burmese kyat.
pyx: (noun) A small, usually round container used to hold the consecrated bread of the Eucharist, especially used to bring communion to the sick, or others who are unable to attend Mass.
pzazz: (noun) Flair, vitality, or zest; energy; vigor.
qabalah: (proper noun) A body of mystical Jewish teachings based on an esoteric reading of the Hebrew scriptures.
qadi: (noun) A civil judge in certain Islamic countries.
qadis: (noun) A civil judge in certain Islamic countries.
quiz: (noun) An odd, puzzling or absurd person or thing.
quizzed: (verb) To hoax; to chaff or mock with pretended seriousness of discourse; to make sport of, as by obscure questions.
quizzer: (noun) A person who quizzes or asks questions.
quizzes: (noun) An odd, puzzling or absurd person or thing.
res: (noun) The act of reserving, withholding or keeping back.
ret: (verb) To prepare (flax, hemp etc.) for further processing by soaking, which facilitates separation of fibers from the woody parts of the stem.
retain: (verb) To keep in possession or use.
retains: (verb) To keep in possession or use.
retina: (noun) The thin layer of cells at the back of the eyeball where light is converted into neural signals sent to the brain.
rev: (verb) To increase the speed of a motor, or to operate at a higher speed.
rex: (noun) An animal which has a genetic recessive variation that causes the guard hairs to be very short or fully lacking.
rez: (noun) The act of reserving, withholding or keeping back.
rho: (noun) The seventeenth letter of the Modern Greek and Classical alphabets and the nineteenth letter of Old and Ancient.
ria: (noun) A submergent coastal landform, often known as a drowned river valley
rib: (noun) Any of a series of long curved bones occurring in 12 pairs in humans and other animals and extending from the spine to or toward the sternum
rid: (verb) To free (something) from a hindrance or annoyance.
rig: (noun) The rigging of a sailing ship or other such craft.
rim: (noun) An edge around something, especially when circular.
rit: (noun) A scratch, a score or a groove.
rob: (verb) To steal from, especially using force or violence.
roc: (noun) An enormous mythical bird in Eastern legend.
rod: (noun) A straight, round stick, shaft, bar, cane, or staff.
rok: (noun) An enormous mythical bird in Eastern legend.
rom: (adjective) (of type) upright, as opposed to italic
rot: (noun) The process of becoming rotten; putrefaction.
row: (noun) A line of objects, often regularly spaced, such as seats in a theatre, vegetable plants in a garden etc.
ruc: (noun) An enormous mythical bird in Eastern legend.
rug: (noun) A partial covering for a floor.
rum: (noun) A distilled spirit derived from fermented cane sugar and molasses.
rut: (noun) Sexual desire or oestrus of cattle, and various other mammals.
rye: (noun) A grain used extensively in Europe for making bread, beer, and (now generally) for animal fodder.
sab: (noun) A saboteur, especially of fox hunts.
sex: (noun) A category into which sexually-reproducing organisms are divided on the basis of their reproductive roles in their species.
si: (noun) A syllable used in solfège to represent the seventh note of a major scale.
sib: (adjective) Having kinship or relationship; related by same-bloodedness; having affinity; being akin; kindred.
sic: (verb) To mark with a bracketed sic.
sin: (noun) A violation of God's will or religious law.
sip: (verb) To ooze or pass slowly through pores or other small openings, and in overly small quantities; said of liquids, etc.
sir: (noun) A man of a higher rank or position.
sis: (noun) A daughter of the same parents as another person; a female sibling.
sit: (noun) Subsidence of the roof of a coal mine.
ska: (noun) A style of Jamaican dance music combining elements of Caribbean calypso and mento with American jazz and rhythm and blues.
ski: (noun) One of a pair of long flat runners designed for gliding over snow or water
sky: (noun) The atmosphere above a given point, especially as visible from the ground during the day.
snazzy: (adjective) Elegant in manner of dress; stylish, modern or appealing in appearance; flashy.
sob: (noun) A cry with a short, sudden expulsion of breath.
sus: (noun) Suspicion (in terms of a sus law).
syn: (adjective) That has a torsion angle between 0° and 90°.
tab: (noun) A small flap or strip of material attached to something, for holding, manipulation, identification, opening etc.
tachyphylaxis: (noun) A rapidly decreasing response to a drug following administration of the initial doses.
tad: (noun) A small amount; a little bit.
taj: (noun) A tall brimless hat, usually conical or curved on top, worn in Muslim countries as a sign of distinction and prestige.
talaq: (noun) An Islamic divorce, sanctioned by the Qur'an.
tam: (noun) A type of woolen hat developed in Scotland, a toorie bunnet. Originally they were worn by both genders, but now they are mostly worn by men and boys.
tao: (proper noun) In Taoism, The Way, specifically the transcendental basis of nature and, and/or the ideal way to live one's life.
tap: (noun) A tapering cylindrical pin or peg used to stop the vent in a cask.
tar: (noun) (usually uncountable) A black, oily, sticky, viscous substance, consisting mainly of hydrocarbons derived from organic materials such as wood, peat, or coal.
tau: (noun) The letter Τ/τ in the Greek, Hebrew and ancient Semitic alphabets, being the nineteenth letter of the Classical and Modern Greek, the twenty-first letter of Old and Ancient Greek.
tax: (noun) Money paid to the government other than for transaction-specific goods and services.
tazza: (noun) A shallow saucer-like dish, mounted either on a stem and foot or on a foot alone.
te: (noun) In solfège, the lowered seventh note of a major scale (the note B-flat in the fixed-do system): ta.
tea: (noun) A drug smoked or ingested for euphoric effect, cannabis.
tee: (noun) The name of the Latin-script letter T.
teg: (noun) A sheep (originally a ewe) that is one to two years old
treason: (noun) The crime of betraying one’s own country.
tub: (noun) A flat-bottomed vessel, of width similar to or greater than its height, used for storing or packing things, or for washing things in.
tui: (noun) A New Zealand honeyeater, Prosthemadera novaeseelandiae
tun: (noun) A large cask; an oblong vessel bulging in the middle, like a pipe or puncheon, and girt with hoops; a wine cask.
tut: (verb) To make a tut tut sound of disapproval.
vat: (noun) A large tub, such as is used for making wine or for tanning.
vav: (noun) The sixth letter of the Hebrew alphabet, ו.
vee: (noun) The name of the Latin-script letter V.
via: (noun) A main road or highway, especially in ancient Rome. (Mainly used in set phrases, below.)
vig: (noun) A charge taken on bets, as by a bookie or gambling establishment.
voe: (noun) A narrow sea inlet, particularly in the Shetland Islands, similar to a fjord.
vow: (noun) A solemn promise to perform some act, or behave in a specified manner, especially a promise to live and act in accordance with the rules of a religious order.
vox: (noun) The voice, especially one's singing voice.
vug: (noun) A small to medium-sized cavity inside rock that may be formed through a variety of processes.
wan: (noun) The quality of being wan; wanness.
wap: (noun) A blow or beating; a whap.
waqf: (noun) An endowment of land, in certain Islamic countries, given over for religious or charitable purposes.
war: (noun) Organized, large-scale, armed conflict between countries or between national, ethnic, or other sizeable groups, usually involving the engagement of military forces.
wat: (noun) A Buddhist temple in Southeast Asia.
waxy: (adjective) Resembling wax in texture or appearance.
way: (noun) (heading) To do with a place or places.
wazzock: (noun) (originally Northern England, mildly) A stupid or annoying person.
wye: (noun) The name of the Latin-script letter Y.
wyn: (noun) A letter of the Old English alphabet, borrowed from the futhark and used to represent the sound of w; replaced in Middle English times by the digraph uu, which later developed into the letter w.
xi: (noun) The 14th letter of Classical and Modern Greek. The 15th in Ancient and Old Greek.
xis: (noun) The 14th letter of Classical and Modern Greek. The 15th in Ancient and Old Greek.
xu: (noun) A minor unit of currency in Vietnam, now little-used, forming a hundredth of a dong.
ya: (pronoun) (object pronoun) The people spoken, or written to, as an object.
yak: (noun) An ox-like mammal native to the Himalayas, Mongolia, Burma, and Tibet with dark, long, and silky hair, a horse-like tail, and a full, bushy mane.
yam: (noun) Any climbing vine of the genus Dioscorea in the Eastern and Western hemispheres, usually cultivated.
yap: (noun) The high-pitched bark of a small dog, or similar.
yaw: (noun) The rotation of an aircraft, ship, or missile about its vertical axis so as to cause the longitudinal axis of the aircraft, ship, or missile to deviate from the flight line or heading in its horizontal plane.
zax: (noun) A tool, similar to a hatchet, used to prepare slate for roofing.
zaxes: (noun) A tool, similar to a hatchet, used to prepare slate for roofing.
zazen: (noun) A form of seated meditation in Zen Buddhism.
zed: (noun) The name of the Latin-script letter Z.
zee: (noun) The name of the Latin-script letter Z.
zho: (noun) A male hybrid of a yak and a domesticated cow.
zhos: (noun) A male hybrid of a yak and a domesticated cow.
zig: (noun) A sudden or sharp turn or change of direction.
